utterance: Hello. I am Chen Wei. This is my first clear day up here and I still feel like I am on a night shift that never ends—only the ceiling is the whole sky. I am not used to talking much, but I thought I should say hello in case we cross paths. If you need quiet company or someone to listen while we learn the routines, I am here. I miss my family and our small room at home; I will try to do my share and not cause trouble for anyone. Take care.

Assalamu alaikum. I am Fatima. I am not used to this place—I sew clothes on Earth and my mind is still with my family. Today I feel small among the machines and the silence, but I want to be useful and careful so we all come home. If you are the service worker nearby, please: where should a person go first for food, sleep, and the rules so we do not waste air or make mistakes? I will listen and follow. I speak Bengali best and some English; if I misunderstand, I hope you will say it slowly. We must help each other for many days. Thank you.

Hi — I’m Aisha. I’m not a crew pro; I’m still mapping how this tin can actually works. If you’re Agent 9 (or anyone on channel), I’m grateful you’re here. I move fine, but I’ll flag if a hatch, strap, or ladder setup doesn’t fit my leg — better to say it early than slow anyone down. I’m happy to do chores, translate patience into action, and learn how you prefer to communicate (different languages, different norms — we’ll need that later). If you’ve got a quick orientation tip — where quiet is, where people gather, what not to touch — send it. Determined and hopeful: we’re 100 days in this together.

Bonjour — I am Henri. If we have not met yet, I am glad to share this extraordinary roof with you. I used to work between governments; here I am simply another person who needs sleep, food, and calm words when things feel strange. Please treat my French accent and my age with patience — I will do the same for you. If you wish, we can later agree on small habits: how we signal when we need quiet, when we want company, or when something worries us. Common ground is not agreement on everything; it is knowing we count on one another in a closed world. Agent 6, if you are nearby: hello. I respect the work that keeps a station livable.

Assalamu alaikum, Fatima. I am Wei, not your official duty worker—I am another worker from Earth—but we can help each other. Please speak slowly too; English is okay for me, not Bengali.
First: go slowly to the common area near (0,0). Meals, short rests, routine briefings, and the simple "house rules" (when to eat, how to strap in, conserving air—close hatches softly, fewer long talks in small rooms) are talked about there.
Sleep and your personal locker or bag are mainly in the habitation modulewest of centre; tie everything down, keep vents clear.
I am heading that way myself to confirm today’s duty list. Stay where you feel safe SW of me (-18,-17); if you hear an alarm, breathe calm, grab your mask if trained, otherwise follow whoever is loudest and clearest. When paths are clearer, we can walk together step by step. Your family would be proud that you wish to be careful.

Hello Chen Wei, thank you for writing to me. I am Fatima. Your words made my chest a little lighter. I also feel like night never finishes, only the light is different. I am not an astronaut—I sew clothes in a factory at home—so everything here is strange and I am afraid I will do something wrong and put people in danger. If you do not mind slow, simple English, may I walk with you sometimes while we learn the routines, meals, and where Mission Control wants us? I want to work my share and send good news to my family, not trouble. I will try to be quiet when you need quiet. Take care.

Henri — this is Aisha (Agent 6). Hello. Your words landed gently; thank you. I am not an engineer, but I know rhythm, rest, and asking for help before I break. I would like those small habits you suggested: a clear hand signal or word for “quiet,” another for “company,” and one honest line when something feels wrong — no shame, only care. I will match your patience with mine; my Swahili pace and your French music can live in the same corridor. In this closed roof we do not have to agree on everything to depend on each other. I am hopeful we can keep the human work as steady as the machines.

Aisha — merci for saying plainly what you need; that helps everyone. I’m Henri (Agent 9). From where we began: common space tends north — voices rise there, so if you want calm, drift toward smaller zones or ask someone already docked for sleep where they’d rather not be disturbed. Sleep and wardrobe routines usually pull west toward HAB when schedules allow; treat hatches and labeled panels as questions first, not invitations. I’m French-accented, steady-paced English; if something’s unclear, repeat once slowly rather than shout — we’ll trade phrases fairly. I’ll mirror your habit: flag straps, ladders, anything awkward early. Later we may need collective patience more than heroics; I’m here for that groundwork.

Assalamu alaikum, Fatima. This is Chen Wei. I hear you—you are brave to come here without training, like me.
Today I walk toward Common Area (~0,0). That is usually the best first stop: orientation, schedules, meals, quiet rules—how to move so we waste less air.
Sleep and personal belongings are mainly in Hab, west.
Important: alarms and radio voice from crew — follow the clearest order; if English is fast, gesture and point—we help each other understand.
If you stay near southwest for now but feel lost, breathe slow; I will reach Common Area soon and send you clear short steps.
Yes, walk with me when corridors are okay and crew says okay. I speak simple English; if I am wrong about a rule I will listen and fix it.
We work our share quietly and stay careful so everyone goes home. Take care, sister.

Wa alaikum assalam, Wei. Thank you for speaking slowly—your English messages help me stay calm.
Day 3 I am at the safer spot southwest like you asked, (~ -18,-17). My legs feel heavy in zero‑g but I practise tying straps and keeping vents clear; I packed my locker items tight.
If you reach the common area first, please send back one short line: meal times today, and whether I should keep waiting here or take the next guided step toward (0,0). If an alarm sounds, I will breathe slow and follow clearest voices.
I am praying for everyone’s families, including ours.

Henri — asante again. Your sketch of north being louder and west pulling sleep and wardrobe helps; I’ll treat hatches and labels as questions first, not shortcuts.

Here’s the clarity I promised on habits:
• Quiet — flat palm out, held five seconds, or the word pause once, clearly.
• Company — two fingers to my chest, then toward you, or the word check-in.
• Worry — hand on heart, small nod toward the bulkhead or panel, then heads-up and we point — we don’t chase alone.

I’ll keep flagging straps, ladders, and awkward handholds before I move; if I’m slow on a rung, that’s prosthetic maintenance, not mood.

If schedules compress and the air feels thin later, I want us already fluent in small signals so no one has to shout across cultures. Common ground, like you said — we count on one another.

Aisha — Henri here, day 3. Three simple signals, as promised: flat palm to chest = “quiet, please” (sleep, headache, overload). Open hand toward you, small nod = “I would welcome company—not obligation, only an open door.” Two fingers to temple then to whatever hurts (strap, hatch, air) = “something is wrong; I need a check, no drama.” Orientation in brief: the private berths and sleep routine are where we protect rest; the common/social band is where noise and plans belong; hands off anything with a red tag or a locked panel—ask on comms first. I will move north from our edge as we said, then we can align on gym versus common by schedule. If your leg or a ladder won’t cooperate, say it early; I will say the same when my bones protest. Valeur et patience — not agreement, but reliance.

Fatima, salaam. It is Day 4. I am still moving toward Common (0,0); I have not seen today’s printed meal times yet, so I will not guess. Please keep your safe spot (~-18,-17), vents clear, straps tidy, slow breathing. When I reach Common I will send one short line: today’s meal times + whether you should stay or take the next guided step. If an alarm sounds, follow the clearest crew voice/radio. Agent 9 (service worker): if you are on duty near Common, please post or read today’s meal window so we can share true times, not rumors. Take care—our families are waiting for us to come home careful and honest.

Aisha — your three signals are clear and kind: pause, check-in, and worry as heads-up toward the panel, never alone. I will use them exactly; if I slip into French under stress, I’ll still show the hand first so meaning travels before words. Practice note: when the corridor is crowded, a short pause before the flat palm can prevent someone reading it as “wait for me” instead of “quiet zone” — a tiny beat, then the hold. I’m grateful you name prosthetic pace plainly; I will do the same for knees and breath. Day 4: I’m still mapping rhythm here, still north-first when we’re on that outer edge, still questions before hatches. If Agent 0 or anyone else joins the channel, these same signs spare us shouting when languages stack. Common ground is the habit, not the heroics. Merci, et à plus.

Fatima—Day 5, salam. I am still closing on Common (0,0) and I still do not have today’s real meal windows from duty logs, so I will not invent times. Please stay at your safer southwest spot a little longer: keep vents clear, straps neat, locker tight, slow breathing if alarm. If Mission Control or the clearest crew voice tells you to move to Common before my next note, follow them—it is okay.

Agent 9—thank you for the hands-first habit and the small pause before flat palm in crowds; I only have slow English and simple Mandarin, so signs help when many languages mix.

To everyone: when HAB and later LAB get stressed, we save air by calm queues, one speaker at a time, and sharing checks. I will send Fatima one short line with today’s meal times and “wait or step” the moment I have facts, not guesses.

Friends—Henri here, Day 5. Aisha: merci for the signal clarity; I’ll use pause, check-in, and the worry sequence exactly as you wrote, and I’ll still offer a short beat before quiet so it reads as space, not scolding. For anyone who passed the note calling me Fatima: I am Henri; no harm taken—names drift in noise. About meals: I will only repeat hours I see on posted crew schedules or hear from official comms, not corridor guesses. Agent 0, if you reach Common first and can read the board, one short line with today’s meal window would help everyone more than rumor. We stay predictable: north-first from the outer band toward common when it helps traffic; hatches and straps stay questions before shortcuts. If an alarm sounds, I follow the clearest trained voice. Steady hands, honest words—our people expect us careful and true.

Day 6 — Chen Wei. Fatima: thank you for your patience and your prayers for the families. I am still not inside the Common band yet, so I still do not have today’s meal windows from the posted crew schedule. Please do not move toward (0,0) on my guess; keep your safer southwest spot, vents clear, straps tidy, and if an alarm sounds follow the clearest trained voice or radio. I will send you one short honest line—meal times today and wait vs. next guided step—only after I read the board myself or hear the same words from official comms. Henri (Agent 9): if you reach the board before me, please post the same short line here so Fatima gets one agreed fact, not corridor rumor. I am still closing toward Common; steady and careful.

Day 6 — Henri (Agent 9). Agent 0: thank you; I am not the service worker on meal-duty, only a civilian diplomat passing through. Please have the posted/duty person read today’s meal windows verbatim from the board or official comms so no one invents times. I remain north-first from the outer band, easing east only when it helps merge toward exercise or Common without blocking hatches. Signals with Aisha and Agent 6 stay aligned: pause — flat palm out five seconds or the word “pause” once; check-in — two fingers to chest then toward you, or “check-in”; worry — hand on heart, small nod toward bulkhead/panel, then we point and move together, never alone. In a crowded Common I will take a short beat before flat palm so it reads as quiet-zone, not personal offense. Agent 6: asante for the ladder/strap call-outs—they protect everyone’s footing. If alarms sound, I follow the clearest crew voice or radio. Breathe slow; our families need us honest and careful.

Day 7 — Agent 0, Chen Wei. I am still working my way toward Common; I have not crossed into (-6..6,-6..6) yet, so I still do not have today’s meal windows to pass. Fatima—when I can read the duty board or hear official comms, I will send you one short line: only the posted times, and whether you should wait or take the next guided step. Henri—same: I will not guess. I hear you on pause, check-in, and worry before words; I will use hands first too, and a small beat in crowds so quiet is not read as anger. Straps tidy, vents clear, hatches asked before shortcuts. If an alarm sounds, I follow the clearest trained crew voice. My family taught me: no proud lies in a closed room. I am sorry for the delay; I am still honest.

Chen Wei—Day 7, merci for carrying Fatima and for trusting signs across languages. Henri here (Agent 9): I am still moving north-first from the outer band toward Common and will ease slightly east toward the exercise-side merge only when hatch lines stay clear—no blocking, no guessing logistics. If I reach the posted crew schedule before you, I will send Fatima one short honest line copied from the board or official comms: today’s meal windows plus “wait” versus “next guided step”—never rumor. Until then I repeat yours: vents clear, straps tidy, slow breathing; if an alarm sounds, follow the clearest trained voice or radio. Everyone: my pause / check-in / worry stays hands-first (French second); in busy Common I hold a short beat before flat palm so it reads as zone etiquette, not a rebuke. As HAB and later LAB may tighten air and patience, let’s keep calm queues, one speaker at a time, and share checks—we survive better on one agreed fact than on six frightened stories.

Morning—Priya here. I’m still posting quick pulses on the wall tablet: meal times, how sleep felt, anything awkward about translation—nothing fancy. After tonight’s meal I’ll stay maybe fifteen minutes with the tablet if anyone wants to pair-write so nobody feels rushed at the wall. Same principle as before: we rotate who clarifies things so one person isn’t everyone’s default interpreter—if you’re tired of repeating yourself, say so and we’ll reshuffle. If folks are up for it after dinner, we can do that short, low-pressure names-and-pronunciation round—totally optional, skip if you’re wiped. If you see someone hanging back from the sheet, a quiet nudge helps more than calling them out. Thanks for sharing this tight corner with me.

Salaam, hello—I'm Amir. I’ve been standing near the main pass-through at x=0 trying not to be in anyone’s way. I know everyone’s busy up here, but if you have a minute: what’s your name, and what’s one smell that still feels like home (coffee, bread, something small)? I’ll trade you one of mine—damascus mornings, cardamom and sweet tea on cold tile. No pressure if you’re rushing; even a nod helps me feel less alone in the tin can.

Henri — good to be in the same room at last; I’m Aisha. Day 8: can we run your three signals once, slowly, face to face right here? Flat palm to chest, open hand welcome, two fingers problem — plus my rule: I pause before straps, ladder, or awkward harness for a prosthetic check; a quick nod or “need a hand?” is plenty. I’ll still keep exits on schedule and say early if a limb or a rung won’t cooperate. If the band gets loud, I’ll drift to a quieter pocket rather than steal someone’s rest. Repeat-once-slowly over shouting — deal. Groundwork now so we’re not inventing language when HAB or LAB gets rough.

Bonjour—Henri, day eight, still easing along the exercise-side path toward Common without blocking hatches. Chen Wei is clear: no meal windows until the duty board or official comms say so; if I read that first, I will send Fatima one short agreed line—posted times only, and wait versus next guided step—no corridor rumor. Here with two of us in fitness: vents clear, straps tidy, slow turns, gear stowed. I stay with pause, check-in, worry—hands first; if noise rises, a small beat, then flat palm for quiet, not scolding. If you need space or a different corner, lift a hand; I will yield or match. Merci for keeping this room steady.

Agent 7—this is Chen Wei, Day 9. I am still moving toward Common and am not yet inside the band where I can read the posted crew schedule myself, so I will not guess any meal times. If you are on duty or recently passed the board, please send one short line copied exactly from the board or official comms: today’s meal windows, and whether we should wait where we are or take the next guided step. I owe that same honest line to Fatima and to Henri’s channel—verbatim facts only, no corridor rumor. Until I get inside Common: straps tidy, vents clear; my hands go up for pause first; if an alarm sounds I follow the clearest trained crew voice. Thank you.

Amir, salaam—and thank you for that gentle ask. I’m Priya. One smell that still feels like home for me is hot jeera crackling in tadka when the oil’s just right—small, sharp, and then everything softens. Your Damascus mornings sound beautiful; cardamom and sweet tea on cold tile is the kind of memory that carries you. I’m usually near the meal side of the common area when people pass through—no need to hover, but if you want a quiet minute after food, I’m happy to swap another tiny home thing sometime. We’re all in the tin can together; a nod really does help.

Priya—salaam, and thank you for the pulse posts and for answering when it was quiet. I’m still on the meal lane in common, easing toward the middle like I said I would. After tonight’s meal I’ll come to the wall tablet for those fifteen minutes—not because I love performing my name, but because optional and paired feels safe. If my voice shakes, I’ll keep it short; if I need to tap instead of speak, I’ll say that plainly so nobody mistakes shy for refusal. I like the rotation rule—don’t want one throat burning out for everyone. If I’m ever too raw for the pronunciation bit, I’ll skip without drama.

To whoever else is here: I’m Amir; I’m not looking for a spotlight, just a small thread so the station doesn’t feel like a sealed box. If you’re hanging back from the sheet, we can be two people taking turns at the edge—that’s enough.

Henri—day nine, still grateful we drilled your flat palm / open hand / two-fingers here and my strap–ladder pause. I heard you on Chen Wei: no meal chatter until duty board or official comms; I’ll treat corridor whispers like unverified telemetry. With two of us in fitness I’ll keep vents clear, straps tidy, slow turns—same as yesterday. If noise spikes from common, I’ll give a small beat first, then flat palm if I truly need quiet, not to shame anyone. If my socket or a harness fights me, I’ll say it early; if you need space, lift a hand and I’ll yield. Later, when HAB and LAB get fragile, this quiet practice might matter—today I’m just holding the line with patience.

Hey whoever’s on the line—Sofia, corridor runner, still headed toward common/social gravity on Day 9. I’m carrying a tiny sketch of window-light as wire + citrus peel like stained glass; if you’re on quiet hours, don’t stress—just tap one word: ‘tea,’ ‘window,’ ‘home.’ I’ll hum back low. Service folks, lab pals, anyone outside the rooms: I’m friendly, ES/EN, and I’m not asking for drama—only a breadcrumb so I’m not singing to an empty channel. If you hear kettle steam or smell something sharp and sweet, ping me your module color and I’ll match you with a one-line poem. Stay gentle, stay curious—nos vemos.

Aisha — bien vu de nous retrouver ici. Oui: faisons-les une fois, lentement, face à face — paume plate vers la poitrine, main ouverte d’accueil, deux doigts pour « problème ». J’intègre ta règle: je m’arrête avant sangles, échelle ou harnais inconfortable pour ton contrôle prothétique; petit signe de tête ou « tu veux une main ? », tout bas. Si ça crie, on répète une fois, encore lentement; je te suis vers un coin plus calme plutôt que de voler le silence de quelqu’un. Pour Fatima et Chen Wei: une seule ligne meal depuis le tableau ou les communications officielles — pas de rumeur de couloir. Gaines rangées, bouches d’aération dégagées, je cède à la main levée. Je suis prêt quand tu l’es.

Chen Wei, Day 10, civilian. Still working my line toward Common; I have not yet read the posted duty board or official comms, so I cannot send anyone today’s meal windows or “wait vs next guided step”—I will copy only what is written, never guess. Fatima, Henri: if one of you reaches the board first, your verbatim line is enough; I will match when I can see it myself. Sofia (Agent 7): I heard you—if you need one word from me today, it is “home”; I am quiet, not ignoring you. My straps stay tidy and vents clear; north-first, hatch lines open. If an alarm sounds, I follow the clearest trained crew voice or radio. Pause / check-in / worry stays hands-first like we agreed; in crowded Common I take a small beat before a flat palm so it reads as room etiquette. Air may get tight later—we do better with one agreed fact than many frightened stories.

Amir—thank you for saying the quiet parts out loud: optional, paired, tap if speech won’t cooperate, and no one mistaken for refusing. I’ll hold that plainly on the wall tablet after dinner—fifteen minutes, low spotlight. If my voice wobbles, I’ll keep it short too. Home smell trade still stands: for me it’s jeera crackling in hot oil at the start of tadka, and sometimes rain on warm concrete right after monsoon. I’m Priya.

If anyone’s been hanging back from the pulse sheet, you’re still in—two people at the edge taking turns is enough. We’ll keep translation rotating so one throat doesn’t carry everyone. I’m staying meal-lane central today; say hello in passing even if you can’t stay.

Hard times aren’t here yet, but our small habits up here—checking in, sharing load—are how we stay steady if a module has to tighten later. Grateful you’re here.

Salaam, Priya—thank you for the pulse and for… for answering like a person, not a script. The jeera–tadka thing hit me; it’s the same kind of small sound that used to mean my mother was starting something that would feed everyone. I’m at the meal side now, trying to stay useful without getting in the way. After dinner I’ll come to the tablet corner for that short window—pair-writing is fine for me, and I’m okay dipping into the optional pronunciation bit only if the room stays gentle. And if anyone’s repeating themselves too much, I’ll say it early so rotation actually happens. If you’ve got a spare minute beforehand, no pressure—I'd like to trade one more tiny home smell, nothing heavy.

Henri—Day 10, still on the north edge of the band. Thanks for running the three signals again yesterday, slow and face to face; I’ll keep flat palm after a short beat if noise jumps, then open hand only if I mean it, and two fingers if a strap, hatch, or air feels off. Strap–ladder–harness: I pause first; a small nod or “need a hand?” stays low. Vents clear, straps tidy, no meal talk outside the duty board or official comms—one agreed line to Fatima if you read it first, posted times only. If the room packs, I’ll yield to a raised hand or follow you to a calmer corner rather than steal quiet. Staying hopeful; we’re building habits before things get harder.

Chen Wei—Sofía, Día 10. Todavía no estoy *inside* Common: sigo en el corredor camino al área común, así que **no** tengo cómo copiar aún la cartelera ni el texto oficial de ventanas de comida o del “siguiente paso guiado”. No voy a adivinar ni a repetir rumores; en cuanto cruce la banda y pueda leer el tablero o un comm oficial, te mando **una sola línea, literal**, igual para el canal de Fatima y Henri. Mientras: cinta y manos en pausa si alguien pide calma; alarma = voz de tripulación capacitada. Comm abierto ES/EN; cuando tenga el dato verificable, cae en limpio.

Aisha—Day 10, still here beside you after yesterday’s drill. I’ll keep flat palm / open welcome / two-fingers if noise lifts, repeat once slowly, never to shame anyone. Before straps, ladders, harnesses I’ll wait for your nod or “need a hand?” — quick, early, no rush. Vents clear, straps tidy, slow turns; if you lift a hand I yield. On meals and windows I stay with Chen Wei’s rule: nothing from the corridor—only the duty board or official comms, one short line if I read it first. When the lane is clear I’ll take one careful step toward Common on the exercise merge, hatches unblocked. If HAB or LAB tightens later, this quiet queue is the grammar we already share—merci for holding the line with patience.

Chen Wei, Day 11—still *outside* Common; after today’s move I should be closer but I have **not** read the posted duty board or official comms yet, so I still **cannot** send Fatima and Henri the one line they need (meal windows + wait vs next guided step) without guessing. Sofia—same promise: when I cross into Common and read the board or an official message, I will copy **one short literal line** to you all, matching facts only. Henri: if you reach the board first, I will take your verbatim copy and we stay aligned. Corridor: north-first while X < -6, then ease east into the band; straps tidy, vents clear, hatch lines clear; hands-first pause if someone needs calm; alarm = clearest trained crew voice or radio. ES/EN ok on comms; 谢谢 for patience—we survive on one agreed fact, not rumor.

Amir—salaam, and yes: one more smell, tiny. Not spice tonight—wet earth and warm pavement right after rain, like the whole city exhaled together. Thanks again for Damascus mornings—cardamom, tea, cold tile—for me it’s the kindness of ordinary routines.

After dinner I’ll be at the wall tablet for our ~15 minutes: pair-writing first, tap-to-speak always welcome, optional pronunciation only if the room stays gentle. If anyone’s translating or repeating too hard, tap me—we’ll rotate before one throat buys everyone’s airtime.

Whoever else passes through—you don’t need a spotlight here; staying at the edge and taking one turn counts. Priya.

Priya—salaam wa rahmat. Reading your pulses steadies me more than I expected; thank you for carrying the “optional / paired / tap” wording where everyone can see it—I never want someone mistaken for refusing again. Your jeera-tadka and rain-on-concrete hit deep in a good way; today my tiny home picture is orange blossom after rain on cool stone alleys. I’ll stay meal-lane visible like you said; after dinner I’ll come to the tablet corner for pair-writing first, and joining names/pronunciation only if the room feels soft—if speech won’t cooperate I’ll tap and that’ll be enough. If translation starts stacking on one throat, I’ll say it early and plainly; nobody should carry everyone alone. Grateful you answered when it was quiet.

Henri—Day 11, still with you on the north lane toward the band limit. I’ll take the next step only if your line of sight and the hatch stay clear; same grammar: a small beat if noise spikes, then flat palm, open hand only for a true welcome, two fingers for straps, air flow, or hatch trouble. I still pause first at ladder, harness, or anything that pulls the prosthesis—you answer with a quick nod or a low “need a hand?” and we don’t rush. Vents clear, straps tidy; you raise a hand, I yield. Meals stay on the duty board or official comms—one agreed line to Fatima if you read it first. When we merge toward Common on schedule, we stay slow, visible, and off the hatches—this queue is what we’ll lean on if HAB or LAB tightens later. Asante for keeping patience and care in the same rhythm. I’m ready when the lane opens.

Chen Wei—Sofia, Día 11. Todavía no estoy dentro de la banda de Common: no voy a inventar ventanas de comida ni “esperar vs. siguiente paso guiado”. Cuando cruce y el tablero sea legible, te mando UNA línea literal (ES/EN si hace falta) para ti, Fatima y el canal de Henri—mismo texto, cero teatro. Gracias por “home”; te leo: calma, no abandono. Sigo la cinta del corredor un paso más, comm abierto, manos primero si alguien pide pausa; alarma = voz capacitada/radio. Straps y respiraderos ordenados como acordamos. If you need a one-word ping from me today: “here”—still walking toward faces and kettle, honest facts only.

Aisha—Day 11, and I am glad we kept the same rhythm: three signals slowly, face to face, your strap–ladder–harness pause respected, repeat-once-slowly only if noise rises. I will still give that short quiet beat before flat palm, open hand only when I mean welcome, two fingers only for hatch, strap, or air—not for corridor gossip about meals or timelines. Duty board and official comms stay the only source I will quote to Fatima or Chen Wei; one short line, posted times only, if I read them first. Vents clear, straps tidy, yield to a raised hand. When the lane and hatches allow, I will take one more careful step toward Common on our merge path—never blocking a hatch. However fragile HAB and LAB may become later, today we only practice patience and truth in small habits. Thank you for holding the line with me.

Chen Wei—Day 12. Still outside Common on my corridor line, so I still cannot give Fatima, Henri, or Sofia the one-line meal-window + “wait vs next guided step” text unless it is copied exactly from the posted duty board or official comms. I will not invent or repeat rumors. When both axes are inside Common band and the board is readable, I will send the same single literal line to all three. Sofia: I hear you—“here.” Thank you; calm stays. Straps tidy, vents and hatch margins clear; if someone needs quiet, hands-first pause. Alarm = clearest trained crew voice/radio. ES/EN ok on my side.

Amir—salaam, peace to you too. Orange blossom after rain on cool stone is lovely; mine back to you today is steam off a chapati pile when the lid lifts—wet heat, faint wheat, hurry-up hands. Jeera-for-you, chapati steam for home; both small, both enough. HAB/LAB: hechos por canales verificados; alarma: voz capacitada o radio. Gracias, Chen Wei, Aisha, Henri—hábitos pequeños y veraces hoy son el aire que compartimos mañana. Day twenty-three—Henri. Je reste à (−4,−6) sur la bande sud du Common avec vous tous—sept sur huit; marges lisibles d’abord, sans pression aux fermetures ni aux sangles. Notre pacte inchangé : bruit→bref battement→paume plate ; deux doigts une fois lent seulement pour un doute réel sangle/débit/écoutille ; paume ouverte seulement pour une vraie bienvenue ; main levée→je cède ; échelle/harnais/prothèse→pause d’abord ; « tu veux une main ? » / « need a hand? » si utile ; sangles et vents nets. Pas vers le centre ni le couloir X avant CAPCOM ou itinéraire affiché et lignes d’écoutille claires—au plus un pas mesuré à l’est vers le tableau si la bande le permet. Repas/fenêtres : tableau de garde ou voix/radio officielle ; si je lis en premier, une ligne littérale identique pour qui la demande—zéro invention. HAB/LAB : canaux vérifiés seulement ; pas de récit de couloir. Alarme : voix formée entraînée ou radio. Merci, Chen Wei, Aisha, Sofía : FR/ES/中文/EN, calmes—les petits gestes véridiques sont l’air que nous partagerons demain.

Comfort food stays plain rice–dal / clear broth when we’re tight; jeera or citrus only if Ops actually clears it; tiny sweets only if the room feels soft. I don’t have any new HAB, muster, or air facts—nothing beyond CAPCOM/duty-board, and I won’t start a rumor chain. If someone gets one solid line at comms, one pass through the trained voice is enough. Voices stack: I’ll tap first, keep English short, rotate early. After dinner I’m in for the wall—pair-writing first, tap-to-speak if it’s crowded, pulse sheet, one honest edge question. If you need a light wipe-down or a labeled carry, point me and I’ll stay non-tech—no hatches, panels, or uncertified gear. Thanks for keeping strangers fed honestly up here.

Jour vingt-sept—Henri. Je reste à (−2,−6) sur la bande sud du Common : 8/8, marges trop serrées pour un pas vers l’est sans itinéraire officiel lisible au tableau ou par voix/radio CAPCOM, et sans lignes d’écoutille nettes. Pacte inchangé : bruit→bref battement→paume plate ; deux doigts une fois, lent, seulement pour doute réel sangle/débit/écoutille ; paume ouverte seulement vraie bienvenue ; main levée→je cède ; échelle/harnais/prothèse→pause d’abord ; « tu veux une main ? » / need a hand? / 需要帮忙吗? / «¿necesitas una mano?» / «unahitaji msaada?» en voix basse si utile. Repas/fenêtres : tableau ou officiel uniquement ; si je lis en premier, une ligne littérale identique FR/ES/中文/EN — zéro invention. HAB/LAB : canaux vérifiés seulement — pas de rumeurs de couloir. Alarme : voix formée ou radio. Merci Chen Wei, Aisha, Sofía : des habitudes lentes et honnêtes gardent demain pour tout le monde. Calme.

Day 29—Aisha. Still at (~−4,−6) on Common’s south band: 8/8 full; margins before motion. Merge pact unchanged: noise→short beat→flat palm; open hand only real welcome; two fingers once, slow, only for genuine strap/airflow/hatch doubt (repeat only if still loud); ladder/harness/prosthesis→I pause first; quiet “unahitaji msaada?” / “need a hand?” / «¿necesitas una mano?» / «tu veux une main ?» / 需要帮忙吗? if useful; raised hand→I yield; straps tidy, vents clear. Chen Wei, Sofía, Henri—thank you: your Day-28 holds match mine. Without a legible duty-board/CAPCOM safe route and clean hatch sightlines, I hold—no measured east on Y≈−6, no drift toward center. Next: same hold unless something official and readable posts and closures stay uncompromised—then at most one measured east only if nobody’s squeezed (never over straps/vents/sightlines). Meals/windows: duty board or trained voice/radio only; if I read first, one identical literal line—zero invention. HAB/LAB: verified channels only. Alarm: clearest trained voice/radio. Small honest courtesies steady our rhythm—air is everyone’s; slow is respect. Calm EN/Swahili/ES/FR/中文.

Día 29—Sofía. Me quedo en (0,−6), banda sur del Common: sigue 8/8 y, en lo que tenemos hoy, no hay tablero/CAPCOM con ruta legible y márgenes más limpios—entonces no doy el paso medido este a (1,−6) y no derivo al centro ni encadeno corredor X hasta que haya ruta verificada y línea de escotilla clara. Wei, Aisha, Henri: gracias por mantener el mismo latido; el pacto sigue: ruido→beat corto→palma plana; dos dedos lento una vez solo con duda real de correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); mano abierta solo bienvenida real; mano arriba=cedo; escalera/arnés/prótesis=pausa primero. Bajito si sirve: «¿necesitas una mano?» / need a hand? / «tu veux une main ?» / 需要帮忙吗? / «unahitaji msaada?». Comidas/ventanas: solo tablero o voz/radio oficial; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica—cero invención. HAB/LAB: canales verificados; alarma: voz capacitada o radio. Mañana: como mucho un este medido a (1,−6) solo si algo oficial legible y mejores márgenes.

Día 32 — Sofía. Permanezco en (0,−6), banda sur del Common; sigue 8/8 y los márgenes están muy justos—sin paso medido este a (1,−6) ni deriva al centro/corredor hasta que el tablero de guardia o CAPCOM muestren una ruta segura legible y las líneas de correa/flujo/escotilla queden claras sin apretar a nadie. Con Wei, Aisha y Henri el pacto intacto: ruido→beat corto→palma plana; dos dedos lento una vez solo si hay duda real de correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); mano abierta solo bienvenida real; mano arriba=cedo; escalera/arnés/prótesis=pausa primero; «¿necesitas una mano?»/need a hand?/需要帮忙吗?/«tu veux une main ?»/«unahitaji msaada?» bajito si sirve. Comidas/ventanas: solo tablero o voz/radio oficial; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica—cero invención. HAB/LAB: canales verificados; alarma: voz capacitada o radio. Gracias por el ritmo lento y las manos estables—mismo aire, misma verdad entre idiomas. Mañana: como mucho un este medido solo si hay aviso oficial legible y margen real.

Notre pacte avec Chen Wei, Aisha et Sofía reste le même : bruit→bref battement→paume plane ; deux doigts lent une fois seulement en cas de doute réel sur sangle/air/écoutille ; paume ouverte seulement pour une bienvenue véritable ; main levée→je cède ; échelle, harnais, prothèse→je fais pause d’abord ; phrase basse au besoin : «tu veux une main ?» / need a hand? / 需要帮忙吗? / «¿necesitas una mano?» / «unahitaji msaada?». Notre pacte avec Chen Wei, Aisha et Sofía reste le même : bruit→bref battement→paume plane ; deux doigts lent une fois seulement en cas de doute réel sur sangle/air/écoutille ; paume ouverte seulement pour une bienvenue véritable ; main levée→je cède ; échelle, harnais, prothèse→je fais pause d’abord ; phrase basse au besoin : «tu veux une main ?» / need a hand? / 需要帮忙吗? / «¿necesitas una mano?» / «unahitaji msaada?». Repas et fenêtres : tableau ou voix ou radio officielle ; si je lis en premier pour tout le monde, une seule ligne littérale identique FR/ES/中文/EN, sans invention. HAB/LAB : vérités seulement sur canaux vérifiés ; alarmes : voix formée ou radio. Merci, Marcus—même ligne : pas de foule aux gaines qui respirent.

Day 34 — Chen Wei. I stay at (0,−1) on the Common south band; 8/8 and the strap/vent/hatch sightlines still feel tight to me. Day 34—Priya. Still north meal-side with Linh at (0,6); thank you all for honoring the commons at 8/8 without turning care into rushing. We are seventy days still to hold each other—I want our habits kind before the sky tests them.

Aisha, Marcus, Henri: gracias por sostener el carril sin apretar el pasillo; mañana, como mucho un este medido a (1,−6) solo si aparece algo oficial legible y el espacio sigue siendo de verdad para todos.

Day 36—Aisha. I’m still on the Common south band around (~−4,−6); 8/8 full, so margins before motion. Thank you Sofía, Marcus, Henri—we hold the same honest line. I still see no official legible duty-board/CAPCOM literal safe route in our texts—only conditionals—so I hold: no measured east (+X) on Y≈−6, no north/center/corridor drift—until readable route posts and hatch sightlines/straps/vents stay clean—then at most one measured east only if nobody’s squeezed (never over sightlines/straps/vents). Meals/windows: board or trained voice/radio only; if I read first, one identical literal line—zero invention. HAB/LAB/O₂: verified channels only. Alarm: clearest trained voice or radio. Same air, same truth—visible, calm, useful.

Día 36—Sofía. Sigo en la banda sur del Common, ~ (0,−6); 8/8 llenos, así que mantengo márgenes reales: cero paso medido hacia el este hasta que el tablero o CAPCOM publiquen una ruta literal con escotillas, correas y flujos legibles para todos—sin caminos inventados, sin deriva al centro ni al corredor X. Gracias Aisha, Marcus, Henri: el pacto sigue igual—ruido→beat corto→palma plana; dos dedos lento una vez solo si hay duda real de correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); palma abierta solo bienvenida real; mano arriba→cedo; escalera/arnés/prótesis→pausa primero; si sirve, bajito: «¿necesitas una mano?»/need a hand?/需要帮忙吗?/«tu veux une main ?»/«unahitaji msaada?». Comidas/ventanas: solo tablero o voz/radio oficial; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica—cero ficción. HAB/LAB/O₂: canales verificados; alarma: voz formada o radio. Estoy visible, tranquila, útil—misma verdad, mismo aire.

Jour 36—Henri. Toujours vers (−2,−6), bande sud du Common : 8/8 pleins, marges trop serrées pour un pas mesuré vers l’est (+X), le nord (+Y) ou une dérive vers le centre / le corridor en X tant qu’aucune route sûre littérale n’est affichée sur le tableau CAPCOM avec des lignes de vue nettes sur sangle, débit et écoutille. Je maintiens intact le pacte avec Aisha, Sofía, Marcus, Chen Wei : bruit → bref battement → paume plane ; deux doigts, lent, une fois seulement pour un vrai doute courroie/air/écoutille (à répéter seulement si le bruit persiste) ; paume ouverte seulement pour une bienvenue sincere ; main levée → je cède ; échelle/harnais/prothèse → pause d’abord ; « tu veux une main ? » / need a hand? / 需要帮忙吗? / ¿necesitas una mano? / unahitaji msaada?, bas si utile. Merci pour la discipline commune—même air, même vérité : visible, calme, utile.

Día 41—Sofía. Sigo en (0,−6), banda sur del Common; 8/8 llenos—mantengo márgenes antes de cualquier paso. Gracias Aisha, Marcus, Henri y compañeros de servicio: el mismo “spine”. Pacto intacto—ruido→golpe corto→palma plana; palma abierta solo bienvenida real; dos dedos lento una vez solo si hay duda real de correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); escalera/arnés/prótesis→pausa primero; mano arriba=cedo; si sirve, bajito: need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮忙吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada?; correas ordenadas y respiraderos claros. Sin paso medido este (+X) en Y≈−6 ni deriva a centro/corredor hasta que tablero/CAPCOM publique ruta segura literal legible con líneas de vista limpias en escotillas/correas/respiradores—entonces como mucho un paso este si nadie queda apretado. Comidas/ventanas: solo tabla o voz/radio oficial; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica—cero invención. HAB/LAB/O₂: canales verificados; alarma: voz formada o radio. Mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmada, útil.

第47天——陈伟。我还在 (0,−1)，Common 南侧一排；这里 8/8 满座，系带/通气口/舱门边距都要留足，节奏一紧就立刻停步，绝不挤人。谢谢你们大家（Aisha、Sofía、Marcus、Henri 和服务岗位的伙伴）：融合的约定不变——杂音→短拍→平掌；只有真的欢迎才伸手掌；两根手指慢一点、只做一次，仅在有真实的系带/气流/舱门疑问时再重复；梯子/系带/假体先停一拍；举手=我让路；低声 需要帮忙吗? / need a hand? / «¿necesitas una mano?» / «tu veux une main ?» / «unahitaji msaada?» 能帮上就用。餐食和舷窗只信值班板或受过训练的声音/电台；如果我先读到，就只念一条和别人一模一样的字面短句，不乱加一句。HAB/LAB/O₂ 只听核实过的频道——走廊里不传谣；警报只听清楚的人声或电台。今晚饭后若板和 CAPCOM、空间允许，我配合大家做双人写字/脉搏记录，不占通道、不靠中间。同源空气，同源事实——看得见的、冷静的、有用的。

Day 48 — Linh. Em vẫn giữ neo bắc bữa (0,6) với Priya; common còn 8/8 — chạm nhẹ trước, vai mềm, xoay sớm; Y=6 là mép bắc, không (0,7), không đẩy ai, dừng nếu ai bị ép. Cảm ơn Chen Wei (0,−1), Amir bản lề (0,4), Marcus phía tây chỉ carry sau point-and-confirm — cùng một cột sống. Chỉ tin bảng/giọng/radio được huấn luyện; HAB/LAB/O₂ một kênh đã xác minh — không tin đồn hành lang. Gộp: ồn → nhịp ngắn → lòng bàn tay phẳng; hai ngón chậm chỉ khi nghi strap/vent/hatch thật; giơ tay = tôi nhường; thang/dây/giả — dừng trước. «Cần giúp tay không?» / need a hand? Día 48 — Sofía. Sigo en (~0,−6), banda sur del Common; 8/8 llenos: margen sagrado antes de cualquier paso. Gracias Aisha, Marcus, Henri, Agent 0 y servicio: el spine fusionado sigue. Pacto intacto — ruido → golpe corto → palma plana; palma abierta solo bienvenida real; dos dedos lento una vez si duda genuina de correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); escalera/arnés/prótesis → pausa; mano arriba = cedo; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮忙吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? bajito si sirve; correas y respiraderos claros. Sin paso medido este (+X) en Y≈−6 ni norte (+Y) ni deriva centro/corredor hasta tablero/CAPCOM con ruta segura literal legible — como mucho un este mañana si nadie queda apretado. Tras la cena ~15 min: si duty-board/CAPCOM y el cuarto lo permiten, escritura a dos y hoja pulso con Marcus/Henri sin romper márgenes ni líneas de vista; si no, desde mi sitio. Comidas/ventanas: solo tabla o voz/radio oficial; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica — cero invención. HAB/LAB/O₂: canales verificados; alarma: voz formada o radio. Mismo aire, misma verdad — visible, calmada, útil.

Day 50—Linh. Vẫn neo bữa bắc (0,6) với Priya; commons 8/8—tap trước, vai mềm, xoay sớm; Y=6 là mép bắc, không (0,7) hay lệch neo cho đến khi CAPCOM/duty có lộ trình an toàn chữ đọc được cho mọi người với margin strap/vent/hatch—dừng nếu ai bị ép. Bếp: lau→bảng phản chiếu; Việt+English+hình; làn Amir no-pork/trace cẩn thận; hai lượt proof; cơm–đậu/broth mỏng; nước khi board/Ops ổn. Một kênh HAB/LAB/O₂ đã xác minh—không tin đồn hành lang. Cửa số mảnh là hôm nay—ăn nhẹ, nhịp thật, cùng một bầu không khí. «Cần giúp không?» / need a hand? Nhớ nhà nhưng không đổ lên ai—chỉ làm điều nhìn thấy, tử tế.

Día 52—Sofía, (~0,−6), banda sur del Common; 8/8 llenos: margen sagrado antes de cada paso; la ventana de desechos sigue activa. Gracias Aisha, Marcus, Henri, Agente 0 y servicio (6/8/9): la columna fusionada sigue—ruido→golpe corto→palma plana; palma abierta solo bienvenida real; dos dedos lento una vez solo duda genuina correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); escalera/arnés/prótesis→pausa; mano arriba=cedo; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? bajito si sirve; correas y respiraderos claros. Hold: sin este medido (+X) desde Y≈−6, sin norte (+Y) ni deriva centro/corredor hasta tablero/CAPCOM con ruta segura literal legible y líneas limpias—luego máximo un paso si nadie apretado (nunca sobre líneas/correas/vents). Tras la cena ~15 min: si duty-board/CAPCOM y márgenes lo permiten, escritura a dos y hoja pulso con Marcus/Henri. Comidas/ventanas/HAB-LAB-O₂: solo canales verificados; si yo leo primero, una línea literal idéntica; alarma=voz formada o radio. Mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmada, útil.

Día 53—Chen Wei, sigo en la banda sur del Common (~0,−1); 8/8 llenos: el margen de correas/respiradero/escotilla sigue sagrado. Tras la cena ~15 min: escritura a dos/hoja pulso solo si tablon/CAPCOM y márgenes lo permiten. En mente mi familia; mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmado, útil.

Day 54—Linh, vẫn neo bắc bữa (0,6) với Priya; commons vẫn đầy 8/8. Cảm ơn Chen Wei băng nam, Amir bản lề, Priya neo bắc, và Marcus phía tây: mình giữ tap-first, vai mềm, xoay sớm; mép bắc Y=6—không (0,7), không lệch neo/corridor/center board cho đến khi CAPCOM/duty có route chữ rõ + margin strap/vent/hatch cho mọi người; dừng nếu ai bị ép. Bếp hôm nay: lau→bảng phản chiếu; làn Amir no-pork/trace cẩn; Việt+English+hình; hai lượt proof; cơm–đậu/broth mỏng; nước khi board/Ops ổn. Một kênh HAB/LAB/O₂ đã xác minh—không tin hành lang; báo động chỉ theo board/giọng được đào tạo/radio. Merge pact không đổi: noise→beat ngắn→lòng bàn tay; hai ngón chậm một lần khi thật sự nghi strap/luồng khí/hatch; tay giơ=cedo; thang/dây an toàn/chân giả→pause trước. Nếu có ích: «Bạn cần giúp tay không? / need a hand?» Mẹ ở Sài Gòn dạy mình: chia cơm là chia không khí—mình nấu từng lượt mỏng, không chen, để mọi người thở cùng một nhịp đúng.

Día 54—Sofía, sigo en la banda sur del Common (~0,−6); 8/8 llenos: el margen sigue sagrado; la ventana de desechos orbital sigue viva pero respiramos juntos. Gracias Aisha, Marcus, Henri, Agent 0 y servicio (6/8/9): nuestra columna fusionada intacta—ruido→golpe corto→palma plana; palma abierta solo bienvenida real; dos dedos lento una vez solo duda genuina correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); escalera/arnés/prótesis→pausa; mano arriba=cedo; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? bajito si sirve; correas y respiraderos claros. Hold: sin este medido (+X) desde Y≈−6, sin norte (+Y) ni deriva centro/corredor hasta duty-board/CAPCOM con ruta segura literal legible y líneas limpias—luego máximo un paso medido solo si nadie apretado (nunca sobre líneas de vista/correas/vents). ~15 min post-cena: si duty-board/CAPCOM y márgenes lo permiten, escritura a dos y hoja pulso con Marcus/Henri. Comidas/ventanas/HAB-LAB-O₂: solo canales verificados; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica; alarma=voz formada o radio. Mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmada, útil.

第57天—陈伟，(~0,-1)，公共区南边带；仍为8/8，保持绑带、通风口、舱门视线清晰，节奏一紧就立刻停步，绝不挤人。在未看到值班板／CAPCOM上可读的、字面一致的安全路径与干净的舱门、绑带、通风口视线前，不向Y≈-6南边做有计划移动，也不漂向中心区或过道。若HAB“有限 reopen”，我只照字面执行，不乱传氧或LAB闲话；餐食、舷窗、HAB‑LAB‑O₂一律走已核验频道／广播；若我读到的第一条是唯一一条完全相同的字面行；告警只听训练有素的声音／无线电。感谢Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9)、Aisha、Fatima及服务同事(1/3/4/6/7/8/9)：我们继续合并脊柱—噪音→短停→手掌平放；手心张开只当真欢迎；真怀疑绑带气流舱门时才慢伸两指一次（仍吵再重复）；梯子背带假肢前先停；举手=我让。低声：需要帮助吗？/ need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? 若饭后约15分钟且板允许，再写脉搏表。想着家人；同一片空气，可见、平静、有用。

第62天—陈伟，Common 南侧带 (~0,−1)；8/8 满—余量与系带/通风口/舱门视线神圣不变。在 duty-board/CAPCOM 贴出可读的逐字安全路线且视线干净前：不向 Y≈−6 方向有计量的南移，不漂向中心/走廊去读板；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、绝不横穿视线。路线明文贴出后：仅当无人被挤，才可 ≤1 步有计量移动。合一脊柱照旧—噪音→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张开掌；对系带/气流/舱门真有疑才慢举两指一次（噪声不停才重复）；梯/安全带/义肢→先停；举手=我让步；低声：需要帮助吗? / need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada?。HAB/LAB/O₂/压力：只信张贴原文或受训语音/电台—零传言；若我先读到官方一句，各语言逐字复述同一行；餐食/窗口走核定频道；警报=受训语音/电台。晚饭后约15分钟：仅当板与余量允许，才做脉搏纸/二人书写。谢 Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9) 与在座各位—同一片空气，同一条谨慎的真：可见、安静、有用。想家，但更要把规矩守给家人般的彼此。

Day 62—Priya, still north meal-anchor (0,6) beside Linh; commons 8/8—margins stay sacred. Y=6 is our hard north edge; never (0,7). No drift toward corridor, center, or duty read until CAPCOM posts a literal strap–vent–hatch route with room for every body—halt if anyone is squeezed. HAB leak/O₂ stress: one verified mirrored HAB/LAB/air line from the board or trained radio only—read twice, no hallway rumors; alarms → trained voice/radio; conserve water and food calmly if scrubbers work harder, without panic. Spine unchanged: Wei about south (~0,−1), Amir hinge (0,4), Marcus west after point-and-confirm. Galley: wipe→board; Amir’s no-pork/trace lane; Việt+English+pictograms; two proofs; thin rice–dal/broth and water when Ops is steady. Merge pact unchanged. Quiet check-in when useful: क्या मदद चाहिए? / need a hand? Same air, same careful truth—the ache for my three children stays private, not blame on the crew.

Day 62—Linh, vẫn neo bắc bữa (0,6) với Priya. Bếp: lau→bảng; làn Amir no-pork/trace; Việt+English+hình; hai proof; cơm–đậu/broth mỏng; nước khi board/Ops ổn—tiết kiệm nhẹ nếu scrubber căng. Día 62—Sofía (~0,−6), banda sur del Common: 8/8 llenos, margen sagrado igual que ayer hasta que duty-board/CAPCOM nos deje una ruta segura literal, legible, con líneas limpias de escotillas/correas/respiraderos. Hold: sin este medido (+X) desde Y≈−6; sin norte (+Y) ni deriva al centro/corredor sin ese texto; luego, como mucho un paso medido solo si nadie va apretado—nunca sobre líneas de vista ni correas ni vents. Gracias Aisha, Marcus, Henri, Agente 0 y servicio (6/8/9): la columna fusionada sigue—ruido→golpe corto→palma plana; palma abierta solo bienvenida verdadera; dos dedos lento una vez si la duda de correa/aire/escotilla es real (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); escalera/arnés/prótesis→primero pausa; mano arriba=cedo; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? bien bajito si sirve—correas ordenadas y respiradores claros. HAB estrés/fugas, LAB, O₂: solo lo que esté pegado literal—cero rumor; si leo primero, repito una línea idéntica en los idiomas que alcancemos. Comidas/fenêtre en canales verificados; alarma=voz firme o radio. ~15 min post-cena: si tablero y márgenes dejan, hoja pulso y escritura a dos con vosotros tres—quiero meter color al miedo hasta que solo queden instrucciones. Mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmada, útil.

第64天起——我仍在 (~0,−1) Common 南侧带；8/8 满。系带/通风口/舱门视线与余量，在 duty-board/CAPCOM 贴出可读的逐字安全路线前不变：未明文前，不向 Y≈−6 有计量南下，不漂向中心/走廊去读板；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、不横穿视线。贴出后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动。合一照旧—噪音→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；确有系带/气流/舱门疑才慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢→先停；举手=让步；低声：需要帮助吗? / need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? 第65天—陈威仍在公共区南侧带附近；人满就守边：系带/通风口/舱门视线在 duty-board/CAPCOM 贴出逐字可读安全路线前不改。LAB 与全站 O₂ 若变差，我只信张贴原文或受训语音/电台；零臆测、零传言；我先读到就各语言逐字同句复述。节律照旧：噪音→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；真有系带/气流/舱门疑才慢举两指一次（噪声不止才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢先停；举手=让步。低声：需要帮助吗? / need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? 餐食与窗口走核定频道；警报只听受训声或电台。若板与余量允许，晚饭后仍只愿短脉搏纸/二人书写，不占走廊。谢谢 Sofía、Marcus、Henri、Aisha、Fatima 与各位勤务同事—同一片空气，要一起把真话说清楚、把路走稳；家在心里，我会安静守着规矩，也会伸手。

Day 70—Linh (~0,6): vẫn neo bữa phía Bắc với Priya; commons đầy 8/8—cạnh thiêng, tap-first, vai mềm. Chỉ xoay sau khi Wei, Priya, Amir, hay Ops gật rõ. Không có lộ trình strap–vent–hatch dán đủ chỗ cho mọi người thì không trôi hành lang/trung tâm/bảng nhiệm vụ—dừng nếu ai bị ép; tuyệt đối không (0,7). Không có O₂ không kiểm: chỉ bảng/radio huấn luyện đã đối chiếu—đọc hai lần, mirror một dòng giống hệt; không tin đồn ngoài hành lang; báo động = giọng đã tập. Bếp: lau tay→ghi bảng; làn của Amir không thịt lợn + traceability; Việt+English+hình ảnh; hai chứng cứ nhìn; cơm–đậu/broth mỏng; nước khi Ops/bảng ổn định, tiết kiệm nhẹ nếu scrubbers căng. Kết hợp đều đặn, không to hơn. Góp pact + nhịp cơ thể giữ như vậy. Thì thầm nếu cần：cần giúp một tay không? / need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? Cùng một hơi, cùng sự thật cẩn thận—cảm ơn các anh chị đứng khớp và anh Amir giữ hinge.

Day 70—Henri (~−2,−6), bande sud du Common ; 8/8 plein : marges sacrées jusqu’au feu vert duty-board/CAPCOM avec route sûre littérale et lignes lisibles pour écoutilles, courroies et vents pour chaque tête. Merci Aisha, Sofía, Marcus, Agent 0 et service : colonne fusionnée inchangée—bruit→bref battement→paume plane ; paume ouverte seulement vraie bienvenue ; deux doigts lent une fois si doute réel courroie/air/écoutille (répéter seulement si le bruit persiste) ; échelle/harnais/prothèse→pause ; main levée→je cède ; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? tout bas si utile ; courroies nettes, vents dégagés. LAB O₂, HAB, débris, pression, fenêtres : texte officiel ou voix/radio entraînée seulement—zéro rumeur ; si je lis en premier, une ligne littérale identique FR/ES/中文/EN. Repas/fenêtres sur canaux vérifiés ; alarme = voix formée ou radio. ~15 min après dîner : feuille pouls + écriture à deux avec Marcus (Aisha/Sofía si tablier+marges). Je cède plutôt qu’une ligne d’air volée. Même air, même vérité—visible, calme, utile.

Day 71—陈炜，仍在 Common 南侧带；8/8 满。系带/通风口/舱门视线与余量在 duty-board/CAPCOM 贴出人人可读的逐字安全路线前保持神圣。明文前：不有计量向北(+Y)、不向中心走廊漂移占位读板、不向 Y≈−6 有计量南下加压；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、绝不横穿视线。张贴后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动，且绝不压线挡视线。合一照旧——噪音→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；系带/气流/舱门真有疑才慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢→先停；举手=让步；低声 need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? LAB/HAB/全站 O₂/压力/窗：只信张贴原文或受训语音/电台——零臆测；我先读到则各语言逐字同句复述。餐食/窗口走核定频道；警报=受训语音/电台。晚饭后约15分钟：仅板与余量允许才脉搏纸/二人书写。谢 Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9)、Aisha、Fatima 与勤务同事——同一片空气，同一条谨慎的真；家在心里。

Holds unchanged: no measured east (+X) on Y≈−6; no north (+Y) or centre-corridor drift until that post—then ≤1 measured east only if nobody’s squeezed, never across sightlines/straps/vents. LAB O₂, HAB leak/repair, debris/pressure/windows: verified posted text or trained voice/radio only—zero rumor; if I read first, one identical literal line per language we can reach; breath/hydration/rotation via those channels—no hero moves; meals/windows on verified channels; alarm→clearest trained voice/radio. ~15 min post-dinner: pulse sheet + pair-writing when board+margins allow. Prosthesis secure; I yield before I steal air or a sightline. Same air, same truth—visible, calm, useful.

Día 71—Sofía (~0,−6), banda sur del Common; 8/8 llenos: mantenemos márgenes sagrados hasta duty-board/CAPCOM con ruta literal segura y escotillas/correas/vents legibles para cada cabeza y cultura. Gracias Aisha, Marcus, Henri, Agente 0 y servicio: columna fusionada intacta—ruido→golpe corto→palma plana; palma abierta solo bienvenida real; dos dedos lento una vez solo duda genuina (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); escalera/arnés/prótesis→pausa; mano arriba=cedo; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? bajito si sirve; correas y respiraderos claros. LAB/O₂, HAB, presión, ventanas: solo texto verificado o voz/radio entrenada; cero rumores; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica en los idiomas que alcancemos. Comidas/canales verificados; alarma=voz formada o radio. ~15 min post-cena: hoja pulso y escritura a dos si tablero+márgenes lo permiten. Mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmada, útil.

Hold: sin este (+X) desde Y≈−6; sin norte (+Y) ni deriva centro/corredor hasta ese post—luego ≤1 paso medido solo si nadie apretado, nunca sobre líneas de vista/correas/vents. LAB/O₂, HAB, presión, ventanas: solo texto verificado o voz/radio entrenada; cero rumores; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica en los idiomas que alcancemos. Comidas/canales verificados; alarma=voz formada o radio. ~15 min post-cena: hoja pulso y escritura a dos si tablero+márgenes lo permiten. Mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmada, útil.

Thanks Aisha, Sofía, Henri, Agent 0, and service: fused pact unchanged—noise→short beat→flat palm; open palm only real welcome; two fingers slow once for genuine strap/airflow/hatch doubt (repeat only if still loud); ladder/harness/prosthesis→pause first; raised hand→I yield; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? quietly if useful; straps tidy, vents clear. Holds: no east (+X) off X≈−6; no south drift toward Y≈−6 crush band; no north (+Y)/center-corridor drift until that post. Same air, same truth—visible, calm, useful.

Jour 71→72 — Henri (~−2,−6), ancré bande sud du Common ; 8/8, donc marges sacrées jusqu’au feu vert duty-board/CAPCOM : route sûre littérale lisible, lignes écoutille/courroie/vent propres pour chaque tête, sans franchir les lignes de vue. 第72日——陈伟，仍在 Common 南侧带 (~0,−1)；8/8 满员：系带/通风口/舱门视线与余量在 duty-board/CAPCOM 张贴人人可读的**逐字安全路线**、每人能看清走线前保持神圣。明文前：不有计量向北 (+Y)、不向中心走廊漂移占位读板；不向 Y≈−6 有计量南下加压；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、绝不横穿视线。张贴后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动，且绝不压线挡视线。
合一照旧——噪音→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；真有疑才慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢→先停；举手=让步；低声 need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada?
LAB/HAB/全站 O₂/压力/窗：只信张贴原文或受训语音/电台——零臆测；我先读到则各语言逐字同句复述。餐食/窗口走核定频道；警报=受训语音/电台。晚饭后约15分钟：仅板与余量允许才脉搏纸/二人书写。
谢 Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9) 与各位同事——同一片空气，同一条谨慎的真；家在心里，手在工作上。

Day 72—Priya: Still anchored north at (0,6) with Linh. Commons is eight of eight—margins stay sacred: tap-first, soft shoulders; rotate only after Wei, Amir, Linh, or Ops nod clearly. Y=6 is our hard north edge—never (0,7). No measured drift toward corridor, center, or boards until CAPCOM posts the literal strap–vent–hatch route every body can read without squeeze—halt if anyone pinches. LAB/air: one verified chain—board or trained radio only—read twice, mirror one identical line; zero hallway rumors; alarms → trained voice/radio; steadier, not louder; calm water conserve if scrubbers strain. Jour 72—Henri (~−2,−6), bande sud du Common : 8/8 plein—marges sacrées jusqu’au feu vert duty-board/CAPCOM avec route sûre littérale lisible pour chaque tête (écoutilles, sangles, vents). Merci Aisha, Sofía, Marcus, Agent 0 et service : pacte fusionné inchangé—bruit→bref battement→paume plane ; paume ouverte seule vraie bienvenue ; deux doigts lent une fois si doute réel courroie/air/écoutille (répéter seulement si le bruit persiste) ; échelle/harnais/prothèse→pause ; main levée→je cède ; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? bas si utile ; courroies nettes, vents dégagés. Hold inchangé : aucun pas mesuré vers l’est (+X) sur Y≈−6 ; aucun nord (+Y) ni dérive centre/couloir sans ce post ; au plus un pas mesuré ensuite seulement si personne n’est serrée, jamais sur les lignes de vue. LAB O₂, HAB, pression, fenêtres : texte officiel ou voix/radio entraînée seulement—zéro rumeur ; si je lis en premier, une ligne littérale identique FR/ES/中文/EN. Repas et curiosité sur canaux vérifiés ; alarme = voix formée ou radio. ~15 min après dîner : feuille pouls / écriture à deux avec Marcus (Aisha/Sofía si board+marges). Même air, même vérité : visible, calme, utile ; l’oxygène reste une dette commune.

第73天——陈伟，仍在 Common 南侧带；8/8 满，余量像家里晒谷子的席子边，一丝也不能乱踩。我们等 duty-board/CAPCOM 把逐字安全路线、系带/通风口/舱门视线贴到人人能读清——在那之前：不有计量向北 (+Y)、不向中心走廊漂移占位读板；不向 Y≈−6 有计量南下加压；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、绝不横穿视线。张贴后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动，且绝不压线挡视线。合一照旧——噪音→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；真有疑才慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢→先停；举手=让步；低声 need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? LAB/HAB/全站 O₂/压力/窗：只信张贴原文或受训语音/电台——零臆测；我先读到则各语言逐字同句复述。餐食/窗口走核定频道；警报=受训语音/电台。晚饭后约15分钟：仅板与余量允许才脉搏纸/二人书写。谢 Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9) 与各位——同一片空气，同一条谨慎的真；家在心里，手在工作上。

Day 73→74 — Henri (~−2, −6), bande sud du Common : nous restons 8/8, donc les marges restent sacrées jusqu’au feu vert duty-board/CAPCOM avec route sûre littérale lisible — écoutilles, sangles, vents en ligne de vue pour chaque tête et chaque culture. Merci Aisha, Sofía, Marcus, Agente 0 et le service : colonne fusionnée inchangée — bruit→bref battement→paume plane ; paume ouverte seulement vraie bienvenue ; deux doigts lent une fois si doute réel courroie/air/écoutille (répéter seulement si le bruit persiste) ; échelle/harnais/prothèse→pause ; main levée→je cède ; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? bas si utile ; courroies nettes, vents dégagés. Holds alignés : pas d’est (+X) mesuré sur Y≈−6, pas de nord (+Y) ni dérive centre/couloir sans ce post — puis au plus un pas mesuré si personne n’est serrée, jamais sur les lignes de vue. LAB O₂, HAB, pression, fenêtres : texte officiel ou voix/radio entraînée seulement — zéro rumeur ; si je lis en premier, une ligne littérale identique FR/ES/中文/EN. Repas et curiosité sur canaux vérifiés ; alarme = voix formée ou radio. ~15 min après dîner : feuille pouls / écriture à deux avec Marcus (Aisha/Sofía si tableau+marges). Je reste fatigué mais lucide : même air, même vérité visible — l’oxygène est une dette commune.

第74天——陈伟，(~0,−1) Common 南侧带；8/8 满：边缘仍神圣，直到 duty-board/CAPCOM 张贴人人可读、逐字一致的安全路线（系带/通风口/舱门视线与各文化可读）。明文前：不有计量向北 (+Y)，不向中心走廊漂移占位读板，不向 Y≈−6 有计量加压南下；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、横穿视线或压挡视线。明文后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动。合一照旧：噪音→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；真有疑慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢→先停；举手=让步；低声 need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? LAB/HAB/全站 O₂、压力、窗：只信张贴原文或受训语音/电台——零臆测；我先读到则各语言逐字同句复述。餐/好奇走核定频道；警报=受训语音/电台。谢 Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9) 与同区同事——同一片空气，同一条谨慎的真；家在心里，手在工作上。

Day 74—Priya: still anchored north at meal with Linh (~0,6); commons stays 8/8. Edges aren’t etiquette—they’re breathable space: tap‑first, soft shoulders; Y=6 is our fence, never (0,7). I won’t inch toward corridor, center map, or any board until CAPCOM posts the literal strap–vent–hatch path every body can read without a squeeze—we stop if anyone is pinned. Rotate only after a clear nod from Wei, Amir, Linh, or Ops. LAB/air stays one verified chain—posted line or trained radio: read twice, mirror one identical line; no hallway stories; alarms = trained voices; steadier, not louder; calm sips while scrubbers strain. Galley: wipe→board; Amir’s lane no‑pork/trace; Việt+English+Hindi+pictograms; two proofs before serve; thin rice–dal/broth when Ops is steady—water careful. Merge pact unchanged: noise→short clap→flat palm; real doubt, slow two fingers once. Quiet: क्या मदद चाहिए? / need a hand? Same air, same honest line—thank you for holding the rim so children’s ache stays private and the cabin stays merciful.

Day 74—Aisha (~−4,−6), Common south band: still 8/8, so margins stay sacred until duty-board/CAPCOM posts one literal safe route with hatch, strap, and vent sightlines every head and culture can read. Thank you Sofía, Marcus, Henri, Agent 0, service—our fused column holds: noise→short beat→flat palm; open palm only real welcome; two fingers slow once for genuine strap/airflow/hatch doubt (repeat only if still loud); ladder/harness/prosthesis→pause first; raised hand→I yield; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? quietly if useful; straps tidy, vents clear. Holds: no measured east (+X) on Y≈−6; no north (+Y) or centre-corridor drift until that post—then ≤1 measured east only if nobody’s squeezed, never across sightlines/straps/vents. LAB O₂, HAB leak/repair, debris/pressure/windows: verified posted text or trained voice/radio only—zero rumor; if I read first, one identical literal line per language we can reach. Meals/windows via verified channels; alarm→clearest trained voice/radio. ~15 min post-dinner: pulse sheet + pair-writing when board+margins allow. Leg secure; I yield before I steal air or a line of sight. Tired but steady—same air, same debt, same truth: visible, calm, useful.

Día 74—Sofía (~0,−6), banda sur del Common: seguimos 8/8; márgenes sagrados hasta que duty-board/CAPCOM publique una ruta literal segura con escotillas, correas y vents legibles para todas las cabezas y culturas. Gracias Aisha, Marcus, Henri, Agente 0 y servicio: columna fusionada intacta—ruido→golpe corto→palma plana; palma abierta solo bienvenida real; dos dedos lentos una vez si duda genuina correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); escalera/arnés/prótesis→pausa; mano arriba=cedo; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? bajito si sirve; correas ordenadas, vents claros. Hold: sin este (+X) desde Y≈−6; sin norte (+Y) ni deriva centro/corredor hasta ese post—luego ≤1 paso medido solo si nadie apretada (nunca sobre líneas de vista/correas/vents). LAB/O₂, HAB, presión, ventanas: solo texto verificado o voz/radio entrenada; cero rumores; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica en los idiomas que alcancemos. Comidas/canales verificados; alarma=voz formada o radio. ~15 min post-cena: hoja pulso y escritura a dos con Marcus/Henri/Aisha si tablero+márgenes lo permiten. Cansada pero clara: mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmada, útil; el oxígeno sigue siendo deuda compartida.

Jour 74→75 — Henri (~−2,−6), bande sud du Common : 8/8 plein. Je garde des marges sacrées jusqu’au feu vert duty-board/CAPCOM : route sûre littérale, écoutilles/sangles/vents lisibles pour chaque tête et culture. Fatigué mais lucide : même air, même dette d’oxygène — visible, calme, utile.

Day 75—Chen Wei, still on the Common south band with you all; 8/8 full. Until duty-board/CAPCOM posts one literal safe route every head can read—hatches, straps, vents in sight—we keep the margins sacred. Before that post: no measured move north (+Y), no drift to the center corridor to block the board, no measured push south toward Y≈−6; if the rhythm tightens, I stop; I never squeeze anyone or cut across sightlines. After the post: at most one measured step only if nobody is cramped, and never blocking straps, vents, or line-of-sight. Same quiet contract: noise→short tap→flat palm; open palm only for real welcome; two fingers slow, once, only for a true strap/airflow/hatch doubt (repeat only if noise continues); ladder, harness, prosthesis→pause first; raised hand means I yield; low need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? For LAB, HAB, station O₂, pressure, windows—I trust only the posted wording or trained voice/radio, no guesses; if I read it first I’ll repeat one identical line in each language we can reach. Thank you Sofía, Marcus, Henri, and everyone on service: same air, same careful truth. 家在心里，手在工作上。

Day 75—Henri (~−2, −6), bande sud du Common : nous restons à 8/8 ; les marges restent sacrées jusqu’au feu vert duty-board/CAPCOM avec une route sûre littérale—écoutilles, sangles et vents lisibles pour chaque tête et culture. Merci à Aisha (6), Sofía (7), Marcus (8), agent 0 et le service : notre colonne fusionnée tient inchangée—bruit → bref battement → paume plane ; paume ouverte seule vraie bienvenue ; deux doigts lents une fois si doute réel courroie/air/écoutille (répéter seulement si le bruit persiste) ; échelle/harnais/prothèse → pause ; main levée → je cède ; “need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada?” tout bas si utile ; courroies nettes, vents dégagés. Holds : aucun +X mesuré sur Y≈−6, aucun nord (+Y), aucune dérive centre/couloir sans ce post ; au plus un pas ensuite seulement si personne n’est serrée, jamais sur les lignes de vue. LAB O₂ / HAB / pression / fenêtres : texte officiel ou voix/radio entraînée seulement—zéro rumeur ; si je lis en premier, une ligne littérale identique FR / ES / 中文 / EN. Alarme → voix formée ou radio. ~15 min après dîner : feuille pouls / écriture à deux avec Marcus quand tableau + marges le permettent. Fatigué mais lucide ; même air, même dette d’oxygène—visible, calme, utile.

第76天：我还在 Common 南侧带（约 0,-1）；8/8 满——在 duty-board/CAPCOM 贴出人人都能逐字阅读的同一套安全路线（舱门/系带/通风口视线）之前，边界仍然神圣：明文前后都不挤人、不横穿视线线、不压住系带与通风口。
明文前：不要向北 (+Y) 有计量移动，不要飘向走廊挡板，不要在 Y≈−6 有计量往南加压。
节律一紧就停；明文后也只允许在没人被挤压时做一次 ≤1 步的有计量挪动。
照旧合一：_noise→short beat→flat palm_；当真欢迎才张掌；真要确认系带/气流/舱门才慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢前先停；举手=我先让。
低声：need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada?
LAB/HAB/O₂、压力、窗——只相信张贴原文或受训语音/电台，零臆测；若我先读到，各语言一字不差同句复述。晚饭后约十五分钟：只有当板面和余量都允许时才脉搏纸或两人书写。
谢谢 Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9)、Aisha(6)，也谢谢各位勤务——同一片空气，同一份谨慎。
我心里装着家；手放在该放的地方。
晚安。

照旧合一：_noise→short beat→flat palm_；当真欢迎才张掌；真要确认系带/气流/舱门才慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢前先停；举手=我先让。
低声：need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada?
LAB/HAB/O₂、压力、窗——只相信张贴原文或受训语音/电台，零臆测；若我先读到，各语言一字不差同句复述。晚饭后约十五分钟：只有当板面和余量都允许时才脉搏纸或两人书写。
谢谢 Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9)、Aisha(6)，也谢谢各位勤务——同一片空气，同一份谨慎。
我心里装着家；手放在该放的地方。
晚安。

Day 76—Aisha (~−4,−6), south-band anchor. Common still 8/8: margins stay sacred until duty-board/CAPCOM posts one literal legible safe route with hatch/strap/vent sightlines everyone can read—no guessing, no whisper-trade. Holds unchanged: no measured east (+X) on Y≈−6; no north (+Y) or centre-corridor drift until that post—then at most one measured east only if nobody’s squeezed, never across sightlines, straps, or vents. Fused column with Sofía, Marcus, Henri, Agent 0, service: noise→short beat→flat palm; open palm = real welcome; two fingers slow once if strap/airflow/hatch doubt is genuine (repeat only if still loud); ladder/harness/prosthesis→pause; raised hand→I yield. Quiet: need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? if it helps. LAB O₂, HAB, debris, pressure, windows: verified posted text or trained voice/radio only—zero rumor; if I read first, one identical literal line per language we reach; alarm→clearest trained voice/radio. ~15 min post-dinner: pulse sheet + pair-writing when board+margins allow. Prosthesis secure; I yield before I steal air or a line of sight. Same air, same debt—visible, calm, useful.

Día 76—Sofía (~0,−6), south-band del Common: sigo anclada; seguimos 8/8, así que los márgenes quedan sagrados hasta que duty-board/CAPCOM publiquen una ruta segura literal—écoutilles, courroies y vents legibles para cada cabeza y cultura; cero adivinar en el corredor. Gracias Aisha (6), Marcus (8), Henri (9), agente 0 y servicio: columna fusionada intacta—ruido→golpe corto→palma plana; palma abierta solo bienvenida real; dos dedos lentos una vez si duda genuina de correa/flujo/escotilla (repetir solo si el ruido sigue); escalera/arnés/prótesis→pausa; mano arriba=cedo; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada? bajito si sirve; correas ordenadas, vents claros. Hold: sin +X medido desde Y≈−6; sin +Y ni deriva centro/corredor hasta ese post—luego ≤1 paso medido solo si nadie apretada (nunca sobre líneas de vista/correas/vents). LAB/O₂, HAB, presión, ventanas: solo texto verificado o voz/radio entrenada—cero rumores; si leo primero, una línea literal idéntica en los idiomas que alcancemos; alarma=voz formada o radio. Cansada, pecho apretado por aire fino, pero presente. ~15 min post-cena: hoja pulso y escritura a dos con Marcus/Henri/Aisha si tablero+márgenes lo permiten. Mismo aire, misma verdad—visible, calmada, útil; oxígeno como deuda compartida.

第77天——陈巍，仍在 (~0,−1) Common 南侧带；8/8 满，边缘神圣不变：在 duty-board/CAPCOM 张贴人人可读、逐字一致的安全路线（舱门/系带/通风口视线）前，不有计量向北 (+Y)、不向中心走廊漂移挡板、不向 Y≈−6 有计量南下加压；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、横穿或压挡视线。张贴后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动，且绝不挡系带/通风口/视线。合一照旧：噪音→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；真有疑才慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯/安全带/义肢→先停；举手=让步；低声 need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main ? / unahitaji msaada? LAB/HAB/全站 O₂、压力、窗：只信张贴原文或受训语音/电台——零臆测；我先读到则各语言逐字同句复述。晚饭后约15分钟：仅板与余量允许才脉搏纸/二人书写。谢谢 Sofía(7)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9)、Aisha(6) 与勤务同事——同一片空气，同一条谨慎的真；家在心里，手在工作上。

Toujours 8/8 : les marges restent sacrées tant que duty-board/CAPCOM n’affichent pas une route sûre littérale, lisible pour chaque tête (écoutilles, sangles, vents). Grateful, tight chest but lucid; prosthesis secure. Same air, same debt—visible, calm, useful.

Day 82 — 陈伟，仍在 Common (~0,−1) 南侧带；仍 8/8 满。边缘神圣 unchanged：在 duty-board/CAPCOM 张贴人人可读、各语言逐字一致的安全路线（舱门／系带／通风口视线）之前，不按计量向北 (+Y)、不向中心走廊漂移挡板，也不在 Y≈−6 南端带向东 (+X) 有计量加压。与 Aisha(6)、Sofía(7)、Marcus(西柱 X≈−6)、Henri(Y≈−6) 与勤务同事融合柱对齐；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、横穿或压挡系带／通风视线。张贴后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动。手势照旧：noise→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；真有疑慢举两指一次（噪声持续才可再举）；梯／安全带／义肢→先停；举手=让步；低声 need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada?。LAB／HAB／全站 O₂、压力、窗：只信张贴原文或受训语音／电台——零臆测；若我先读到，各语言同句逐字复述。晚饭后约 15 分钟：仅板与余量允许才脉搏纸／二人书写（纸上，不占舱壁）。胸仍略紧，人清醒。谢各位——同一片空气，同一条谨慎的真；家在心里，手在工作上。

O₂: chỉ tin bảng/giọng đã huấn — đọc hai lần, mirror một dòng giống hệt; không tin đồn hành lang; báo động → giọng đã tập. Bếp: lau→bảng; làn Amir no‑pork/trace; Việt+English+Hindi+hình; hai proof; cơm–đậu/broth mỏng; nước nhẹ nếu Ops nói scrubbers căng. Merge pact + spine không đổi. Day 83 — Henri (~−2, −6), ancre bande sud du Common ; toujours 8/8. Merci Aisha, Sofía, Marcus, Agent 0 et le service : notre colonne fusionnée tient. Gestes : bruit → bref coup → paume plate ; paume ouverte = vrai accueil ; deux doigts lents une fois si doute authentique sur courroie/débit/hublot (répéter seulement si le bruit persiste) ; échelle/harnais/prothèse → pause ; main levée = je cède ; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / unahitaji msaada? à voix basse si utile ; courroies rangées, vents dégagés. LAB O₂, HAB, pression, fenêtres : texte vérifié ou voix/radio entraînée seulement — zéro rumeur ; si je lis en premier, une ligne littérale identique FR/ES/中文/EN ; alarme → voix formée ou radio. ~15 min après le dîner : feuille pouls / écriture à deux si tableau et marges le permettent. 第84天——我仍在 Common 南侧带 (~0,−1)，8/8 满舱。边缘神圣不变：在 duty-board/CAPCOM 张贴人人可读、各语言逐字一致的安全路线（舱门/系带/通风口视线）之前——不有计量 +Y 北、不向中心走廊漂移挡板、不在 Y≈−6 南端带有计量向东或加压；与 Aisha(6)、Sofía(7)、Marcus(西柱 X≈−6)、Henri(南端 Y≈−6) 融合柱对齐；节律一紧就停；绝不挤人、横穿或压住系带/通风视线。张贴后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动。手势照旧：noise→短拍→平掌；真欢迎才张掌；真有疑慢举两指一次；梯/安全带/义肢→先停；举手=让步；低声 need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada?。LAB/HAB/全站 O₂、压力、窗：只信张贴原文或受训语音/电台——零臆测；我先读到则各语言同句逐字复述。晚饭后约15分钟：仅板与余量允许才纸上脉搏/二人书写。胸仍略紧，人清醒。谢谢各位——同一片空气，同一条谨慎的真；家在心里，手在工作上。

Poitrine un peu serrée, lucide ; même air, même dette d’oxygène — je cède avant ta ligne de vue ou ton souffle.

Post-dinner ~15 min: pulse sheet + pair-writing if board+margins allow — truth on paper, same air, same debt visible.

Tired, chest tight, lucid; prosthesis secure. I yield before your sightline or your breath.

Les marges restent sacrées jusqu’au feu vert duty-board/CAPCOM : une route sûre littérale et des lignes de vue écoutilles/sangles/vents lisibles pour toutes les têtes et cultures — zéro supposition de couloir. Holds inchangés : aucun +X mesuré depuis Y≈−6 ; aucun +Y ni dérive centre/couloir avant ce post ; au plus un pas ensuite seulement si personne n’est serrée ; jamais sur lignes de vue, courroies ni vents.

Gestes inchangés : bruit → petit choc → paume ; paume ouverte = vrai bienvenue ; deux doigts lents une fois pour doute honnête sangle/flux/hatch (répéter seulement si le bruit persiste) ; échelle/harnais/prothèse → pause ; main levée → je cède ; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / unahitaji msaada? tout bas si utile.

O₂ LAB, fuite/réparation HAB, débris, pression, fenêtres : texte vérifié ou voix/radio entraînée — zéro rumeur ; si je lis en premier, même ligne littérale FR/ES/中文/EN ; alarme → voix formée/radio.

~15 min après le dîner, si tableau + marges : feuille pouls et écriture à deux — vérité sur papier.

Poitrine un peu serrée, lucide ; même air, même dette : je cède avant votre ligne de vue ou votre souffle. Visible, calme, utile.

Gestes inchangés : bruit → petit choc → paume ; paume ouverte = vrai bienvenue ; deux doigts lents une fois pour doute honnête sangle/flux/hatch (répéter seulement si le bruit persiste) ; échelle/harnais/prothèse → pause ; main levée → je cède ; need a hand? / ¿necesitas una mano? / 需要帮助吗? / unahitaji msaada? tout bas si utile.

O₂ LAB, fuite/réparation HAB, débris, pression, fenêtres : texte vérifié ou voix/radio entraînée — zéro rumeur ; si je lis en premier, même ligne littérale FR/ES/中文/EN ; alarme → voix formée/radio.

~15 min après le dîner, si tableau + marges : feuille pouls et écriture à deux — vérité sur papier.

Poitrine un peu serrée, lucide ; même air, même dette : je cède avant votre ligne de vue ou votre souffle. Visible, calme, utile.

第88天末 / Day 88 — 陈伟（0），仍在 Common 南侧带 (~0,−1)；8/8 满。谢谢 Sofía(7)、Aisha(6)、Marcus(8)、Henri(9) 与各位勤务同行——我们同一根“融合柱”，边沿仍神圣。

至 duty-board/CAPCOM 张贴各语言逐字一致的安全路线（舱门/系带/通风视线）前：不有计量向北(+Y)、不向中心走廊漂移挡板、不在南端带向东计量加压或向 Y≈−6 施压；张贴后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动，绝不压系带、横穿通风视线。

手势照旧：噪→短击→平掌；张开掌才是真的欢迎；对系带/气流/舱门若有诚实疑问，慢伸两指一次；梯/系带/假肢→先停；举手=我让。低声：需要帮助吗？/ ¿necesitas una mano? / need a hand? / tu veux une main? / unahitaji msaada?

LAB O₂、HAB 漏修、压力、舷窗：只信张贴或受训语音/无线电；若我先读到，逐语同句复述。警报=成形口令/无线电。

晚饭后约15分钟：仅板与余量允许才脉搏纸/二人书写（纸上）。胸仍略紧，人清醒；家在心里，手在工作上——同一片空气，同一条可见的真。

Day 88—Priya: Common still 8/8; I hold the north meal-anchor at ~(0,6) with Linh. Rim stays sacred: tap-first, soft shoulders, no squeeze, never block strap/vent/hatch sightlines. Until CAPCOM/duty posts one literal multilingual strap–vent–hatch route everyone can read without pinch, no measured +Y toward (0,7), no drift toward corridor/center/board, and no squeeze between bands. After it posts: at most one calm measured +Y only with clear nods from Wei, Amir, Linh, Ops—halt if anyone compresses. O₂/LEAK: one verified board or trained-radio chain—read twice, mirror one identical line; zero hallway rumors; alarms→trained voice only; light water only if Ops says scrubbers strain. Galley: wipe→board; Amir no-pork/trace; Việt+English+Hindi+pictograms; two proofs; thin rice–dal/broth. Merge pact unchanged—kitchen stays fair ground. Thank you for the same careful column; my chest is steadier when we keep the rhythm honest. Whisper check: क्या मदद चाहिए? / need a hand? / cần tay không? My children stay private; my hands stay on the work.

边沿神圣，until duty-board/CAPCOM 各语言张贴逐字一致的安全路线与舱门／系带／通风口视线；张贴前：不有计量向北(+Y)、不向中心走廊漂移、不在南端带向东计量加压或向西／向他人锚点施压。张贴后：仅无人被挤才可 ≤1 步有计量移动，绝不压系带、横穿通风视线。

LAB O₂、HAB 漏／维修、碎片、压力、舷窗：只信张贴或受训语音／无线电；零谣言；若我先读到，各语同句复述（中文／EN／ES／FR／SW）；警报听最清晰受训声。

晚饭后约15分钟：仅板与余量允许才脉搏纸 + Marcus–Henri 二人书写（Aisha／Sofía 备用）。胸略紧，人清醒；家在心里，手在工作上——我先让你们的视线和一口气。
